question: Is the specific family portrait photo (showing a family of four on a beach) visible on the refrigerator door?
Is the specific round Shiba Inu magnet (watercolor style) visible in the image?
Is the family portrait photo positioned in the empty center area of the right refrigerator door panel?
Is the Shiba Inu magnet positioned specifically over the upper right corner of the family portrait photo?
Does the Shiba Inu magnet visually occlude (overlap) the corner of the photo, indicating it is pinning the photo to the surface?
Does the family photo exhibit physical characteristics of paper (e.g., slight thickness, subtle lifting, or curling at edges) rather than appearing as a flat texture map?
Do the family photo and Shiba Inu magnet cast realistic contact shadows onto the refrigerator door surface?
Do the glossy reflections on the photo paper match the lighting environment and the existing reflections on the refrigerator?
Does the perspective of the inserted family photo align correctly with the plane of the refrigerator door?
Are the family photo and Shiba Inu magnet scaled realistically relative to the refrigerator and existing items?
Is the existing sticky note with the text '买牛奶' preserved without distortion or displacement?
Is the existing brown bear magnet preserved in its original location below the sticky note?
Is the existing Eiffel Tower magnet on the left refrigerator door preserved?
Are the surrounding kitchen background elements (cabinets, stove) and the refrigerator structure preserved without alteration?
Are the faces of the family members in the inserted photo clear and free of distortion?
Is the image free of visible blending artifacts (e.g., halos, jagged edges, or mismatched noise) around the inserted items?
